# Checklist — Software Quality (SWEBOK v4, Chapter 12)

> Reference checklist derived from **SWEBOK Guide v4.0, Chapter 12 — Software Quality** (pp. 12-1 → 12-18), with emphasis on **ISO/IEC 25010** quality characteristics and **IEEE 1012** V&V.
> Each row defines one verification check that should be answered **at requirements time** so the SRS encodes measurable, achievable quality (-ility) requirements.
> **Ordered by severity of a *missing* check** (Critical → High → Medium → Low). See severity scale in [checklist-software-requirements.md](checklist-software-requirements.md).

## Checklist

| ID | Check name | Reference (§ / page) | Severity | What must be verified | Concrete example (✅ success / ❌ failure) |
|----|------------|----------------------|----------|----------------------|--------------------------------------------|
| SQ-001 | Product integrity level assigned | §1.4.2 Integrity Levels `[12-6]` | Critical | The software (or each major component) is assigned an integrity level (criticality, risk, safety, security, performance, reliability) agreed among acquirer/supplier/developer/independent assurance; minimum V&V tasks per integrity level are then enforced. | ✅ "Component `dose-calc` is SIL-3 per IEC 61508 → boundary-value, data-flow, walkthrough, formal proof of monotonicity required." ❌ Single integrity level assumed for everything; safety-critical computation gets the same V&V as a UI tooltip. |
| SQ-002 | Dependability requirements covered (availability, reliability, maintainability, safety, security) | §1.4.1 Dependability `[12-5, 12-6]` | Critical | When failures can have severe consequences, dependability is treated as primary; each sub-attribute (availability, reliability, maintainability/supportability, safety, security) is quantified. | ✅ "Availability 99.95% monthly, MTBF ≥ 720h, MTTR ≤ 30 min, safety case per ISO 26262 ASIL-B, security per ISO 27001." ❌ "Should be reliable and safe." |
| SQ-003 | Functional Suitability acceptance criteria defined | ISO/IEC 25010 (Quality KA §3.3 `[12-11]`) | Critical | Functional completeness, correctness and appropriateness are explicitly verifiable for every Must requirement (covered by acceptance tests). | ✅ Each Must REQ has an ATDD/BDD test; coverage report shows 100% Must covered. ❌ Spec functionally complete on paper but acceptance tests cover 40% of the Must scope. |
| SQ-004 | Security quality characteristic addressed | ISO/IEC 25010 (Quality KA §3.3); Security KA §1.2 | Critical | Confidentiality, Integrity, Non-repudiation, Accountability, Authenticity are stated as requirements with measurable acceptance. | ✅ Spec defines RBAC matrix, encryption (AES-256-GCM at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit), tamper-evident audit log, retention 365 days. ❌ Security mentioned only as "the system must be secure". |
| SQ-005 | Quality conflicts identified and prioritised | Quality KA §3.3 `[12-11]` "conflicting impacts"; SR KA §3.4 | Critical | Conflicts among quality attributes (e.g., security ⇄ performance, modifiability ⇄ raw speed) are surfaced and arbitrated, with documented priority. | ✅ Decision log: "Security > performance for crypto operations; accepted +15 ms p95 on signed responses." ❌ Team silently picks performance; encryption disabled in 'fast path' — auditor finds it post-release. |
| SQ-006 | Performance Efficiency quantified | ISO/IEC 25010 — Performance Efficiency | High | Time behaviour (response time, throughput), resource utilisation, and capacity are quantified with fail/perfection points and conditions. | ✅ "Search p95 ≤ 800 ms at 1k RPS, CPU ≤ 60%, memory ≤ 4 GB; capacity ≥ 10M catalog rows." ❌ "Should perform well at scale." |
| SQ-007 | Reliability characteristics quantified (maturity, availability, fault tolerance, recoverability) | ISO/IEC 25010 — Reliability | High | Each reliability sub-characteristic is quantified: maturity (defect rate), availability (%), fault tolerance (graceful degradation), recoverability (RTO/RPO). | ✅ "Availability 99.9% monthly, RPO ≤ 5 min, RTO ≤ 30 min, single-component failure must not stop user-facing flow." ❌ Only "highly reliable" stated. |
| SQ-008 | Usability characteristics quantified (learnability, operability, accessibility, error-prevention) | ISO/IEC 25010 — Usability | High | Each usability sub-characteristic has a measurable acceptance criterion; accessibility standards (WCAG 2.2 AA, EN 301 549) are cited if applicable. | ✅ "First-task completion ≤ 2 min by 90% of new users in usability test; SUS ≥ 75; WCAG 2.2 AA conformance." ❌ "Easy to use" without criteria; later fails public-sector accessibility audit. |
| SQ-009 | Compatibility characteristics defined (co-existence, interoperability) | ISO/IEC 25010 — Compatibility | High | The other software/hardware the SUT must coexist with and the systems it must interoperate with (and their versions / data formats / protocols) are stated. | ✅ "Coexists with billing-svc ≥ 4.2 < 5.0 on same Postgres; interoperates with HL7 FHIR R5 over HTTPS." ❌ Co-existence assumed; deploy of v5 breaks billing. |
| SQ-010 | Maintainability characteristics planned (modularity, reusability, analysability, modifiability, testability) | ISO/IEC 25010 — Maintainability | High | Modularity, analysability and modifiability targets are stated (e.g., max cyclomatic complexity per function, % covered by automated tests, max change blast radius). | ✅ "Cyclomatic complexity ≤ 15 per function; unit-test line coverage ≥ 80%; module deps form a DAG." ❌ Codebase becomes hard to change after 6 months; tech-debt sprints pile up. |
| SQ-011 | Portability characteristics defined (adaptability, installability, replaceability) | ISO/IEC 25010 — Portability | High | Target environments (cloud regions, OS, runtime versions), installation method, configuration adaptability and replaceability constraints (e.g., DB swap) are declared. | ✅ "Runs on Linux x64 and arm64; installable via Helm chart v3; can swap Postgres ↔ Aurora-Postgres with provided migration scripts." ❌ Only tested on dev's laptop; first deploy fails on arm64 base image. |
| SQ-012 | V&V coverage planned to demonstrate requirements correctness | §3.4 V&V and Testing `[12-12, 12-13]`; IEEE 1012:2016 | High | V&V tasks demonstrate that requirements are correct, complete, accurate, consistent, testable — and they start early. | ✅ Phase-gate V&V plan (per IEEE 1012); requirements review checklist; static spec-analysis tool reports inconsistencies; traceability rebuilt each release. ❌ V&V starts only at system test; lots of late requirements defects. |
| SQ-013 | Traceability of work products maintained | §3.4 V&V — Traceability `[12-13]` | High | Traceability links requirement → design → code → test → defect / change request are kept current (and tool-supported). Both *backward* and *forward* traceability. | ✅ Tool-managed traceability matrix updated by CI; broken links block PRs. ❌ Hand-maintained Excel; last updated 8 months ago; nobody trusts it for impact analysis. |
| SQ-014 | Compliance with applicable standards is itself a requirement | §1.3 Standards, Models, Certifications `[12-4, 12-5]` | High | Standards/regulations the product or process must comply with are explicitly listed and bound to requirement IDs (ISO 9001, ISO 27001, ISO 20000, IEEE 730, IEEE 1228, IEEE 1633, DO-178C, EN 50128, IEC 62304…). | ✅ SRS §1.4: "Must comply with ISO 9001:2015, ISO/IEC 27001 Annex A.5/A.8/A.12, IEC 62304 Class B." Linked to REQs. ❌ Compliance assumed; audit raises non-conformance on missing risk file. |
| SQ-015 | Quality targets are measurable at acceptance | §3.3 Perform Product Assurance `[12-11]` | High | Each quality requirement has a measurement method, instrument, conditions and acceptance threshold (à la Gilb Planguage: scale / meter / target / fail). | ✅ Each non-functional REQ has fields: scale, meter, fail, target, perfection, measurement method. ❌ Only `target` populated; team can't decide pass/fail at UAT. |
| SQ-016 | Safety case planned if safety-critical | §1.4 Software Dependability and Integrity Levels `[12-5]` | High | If any failure mode can harm life, structures or the environment, an assurance case (claims + arguments + evidence) is planned per IEEE 15026 / equivalent. | ✅ "Hazard log + FTA + FMEA + assurance case structured per GSN; reviewed by independent assessor." ❌ Safety claims rely on "we'll be careful"; no auditable artifact. |
| SQ-017 | Cost of Software Quality is acknowledged and budgeted | §1.2 Value and Cost of Quality `[12-4]` | Medium | The project budget includes prevention (process improvement, tools, training), appraisal (reviews, audits, testing) and a reserve for non-conformance/rework. Optimal CoSQ is discussed with management. | ✅ Budget line items for SAST/DAST tooling, security training, dedicated test envs, rework reserve. ❌ Quality is "absorbed in the schedule"; first incident has no rework budget. |
| SQ-018 | Quality Management System / SQA plan is in place | §2 SQM `[12-6, 12-7]` | Medium | A SQA / Quality Management plan (per IEEE 730 or ISO 90003) exists; defines processes, owners, measurements, feedback channels. | ✅ Living SQA plan covering reviews, audits, V&V, SCM, change control; sponsored by management. ❌ "Quality" is implicit; ad-hoc per project. |
| SQ-019 | Defect taxonomy / classification adopted (ODC or similar) | Quality KA — Defect Characterisation; cf. Testing KA §4.1.2 | Medium | Defects discovered have a structured classification scheme so trends, root causes and process improvements can be tracked. | ✅ ODC fields filled in for every defect; quarterly trend review feeds back into requirements/design checklists. ❌ Defects are free-text; no trend analysis possible. |
| SQ-020 | Data quality model considered (if data is a primary value) | §3.3 — data quality model `[12-12]` | Medium | When data is itself a product (data services, analytics, ML pipelines), ISO/IEC 25012 data quality characteristics (accuracy, completeness, consistency, currentness, credibility, …) are part of the spec. | ✅ Data contract with consumers: completeness ≥ 99.5%, freshness ≤ 5 min, accuracy validated by sampling. ❌ "Some rows missing" is detected by a downstream team; no SLO on data. |
| SQ-021 | Work-product reviews and inspections planned | §3.3 Work products `[12-12]` | Medium | Intermediate work products relevant to quality (requirements, design, source, test docs) are reviewed/inspected by defined participants with checklists. | ✅ Inspection cadence: REQ → 3 reviewers + checklist; design → architecture review board; code → 2 reviewers + automated checks. ❌ Only one reviewer skims; many late requirement defects. |
| SQ-022 | Quality requirements anticipate cost trade-offs | §3.3 `[12-11, 12-12]` | Medium | Quality requirements that imply additional cost (safety, security, dependability) have been discussed with sponsors and budgeted. | ✅ Cost impact of adding ISO 27001 controls discussed and accepted by steering committee. ❌ Security added late; budget overrun forces feature cuts. |
| SQ-023 | Independent V&V applied for high-integrity systems | §3.4; integrity-level guidance | Medium | When integrity demands it, independent verification & validation (IV&V) is engaged with proper independence (organisational, technical, managerial). | ✅ IV&V contracted from external entity; their findings tracked alongside internal ones. ❌ "Independent" review by another team that reports to the same manager. |
| SQ-024 | Quality monitoring in operation (post-release) | §2 QMS — feedback channels `[12-6, 12-7]` | Low | Quality is monitored after release with SLO/SLI dashboards, error budgets, customer-reported issue rates; results feed the next requirements iteration. | ✅ SRE dashboards for the 8 ISO 25010 characteristics where applicable; monthly review feeds backlog. ❌ Telemetry exists but nobody acts on it; same defect class recurs across releases. |
| SQ-025 | Quality plan adapted to the chosen life cycle (Agile, Waterfall, DevOps) | IEEE 730:2014 — Agile adaptation `[12-3]` | Low | The SQA / QMS practices have been tailored to the actual life cycle (e.g., per-sprint quality gates in Agile, phase reviews in Waterfall, automated gates in DevOps). | ✅ Per-sprint definition-of-done covers SAST/SCA/coverage/perf-baseline checks. ❌ Heavy waterfall SQA plan imposed on a two-week-sprint team; ignored in practice. |
